page: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. It features 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. There are no dedicated GPUs on this instance, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2938.58 in stress-ng tests, which places it in the top 25% of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance is average, with a stress-ng score of 23323.12 and a Passmark CPU score of 18066.14. Memory bandwidth is also average, measuring 123.16 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 72.22 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform at moderate levels, with a Passmark encryption score of 9753.51 and single-threaded gzip compression throughput of 27.42 GB/sec.

This server is suited for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, such as web serving, database operations, and lightweight text processing. It achieved a static web serving throughput of 8.57 GB/sec and 15.55 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. However, the shared CPU allocation and a weak prompt processing speed of 25.18 tokens/sec for medium 7B LLM models represent key performance tradeoffs, making it less suitable for heavy machine learning or large-scale AI inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 8 shared vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server delivers strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2938.58 in stress-ng tests. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 18066.14 and large-block read speeds of 123.16 GB/sec. The instance includes one public IPv4 address and lacks GPU acceleration. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-focused workloads such as static web serving, Redis database operations, and basic data processing, though its shared CPU allocation and weak 7B LLM prompt processing performance limit its suitability for demanding artificial intelligence workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core CPU performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-8c-16gb-150s-amd features 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 150 GB NVMe storage, optimized for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC-powered compute instance featuring eight shared vCPUs and local NVMe storage for balanced web serving and database workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory and 150 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance placing in the top 25%
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Best suited for static web serving, Redis, and database operations
